expected_properties: Q returns to 0 after reset deassertion regardless of prior state
additional_cases.goal: Verify that the asynchronous active‑low reset reliably forces Q to zero even when asserted mid‑cycle
notes: Reset is active‑low and may be asserted for multiple clock cycles
stimulus_intent: Assert rst_n low for one clock, then release and observe Q stabilization
timing_assumptions: Allow at least one clock after reset release before checking Q

expected_properties: Q remains 0 while rst_n is held low
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that no counting occurs while the reset is asserted
notes: Ensures the design does not inadvertently toggle Q during reset
stimulus_intent: Hold rst_n low for several consecutive clock cycles
timing_assumptions: Observe Q across multiple clock edges while reset is asserted

expected_properties: Q follows the Johnson counter rule each rising edge of clk after reset release
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate correct Johnson counting behavior over multiple cycles
notes: Observes both increment and decrement phases of the 64‑bit Johnson counter
stimulus_intent: Release reset and allow the counter to evolve for a conservative number of clock edges
timing_assumptions: Observe at least 128 clock cycles to cover a full Johnson sequence
